results.title: Behind the curtain of the world of college commitment graphics
highlights: How X and Instagram edits for Delaware recruits get made   ...    When high school and college athletes share the news on social media this week of their next destination, each announcement will likely be accompanied by a stylized graphic that shows them in the jersey they will wear next. What began as a practice tailored to the top teenage athletes in the country has made its way everywhere on X, Instagram and the like. In 2025, you can't commit to a university or finalize your decision to transfer –no matter the level –without first reaching for a few style points and kudos. Expect to see athletes dropped onto their future fields, walking their new Main Street or posed with a mascot on graphics coming to your social media feeds Wednesday on National Signing Day. It's the second of two signing days for football. The first was on Dec. 4. A commitment graphic made by WP Graphics for University of Delaware kicker Skyler Sholder. Sholder graduated from St.   ...    It will be a busy week for Ryan Polter, a 21-year-old finance student at Ohio State. Polter is the man behind WP Graphics, a leader in the college commitment graphic space. His company's graphics are among the most used by Delaware   ...    After about a year of offering graphics for free, he developed a big enough following to start charging and brought on freelancers he found through Instagram to help fill requests in the busiest parts of the year.   ...    They work off templates for consistency –there's a style athletes have come to expect that emanates from Tipton and   ...    Most of Polter's work unfolds in the direct messages of his X and Instagram accounts. He fills any moment of free time by messaging with clients from across the country. The most requested type of graphic is one to announce which school the athlete is committing to. Some will post graphics earlier in the recruitment process with the logos of the schools they have narrowed their decision to or the star rating they have received from a recruiting service. Polter asks athletes for a high-quality photo, the name of the school and if there is any other details they want included. Polter's standard graphic, which includes a jersey swap, costs $50. He also offers a $55 bundle that includes the   ...    The jersey swap from the beginning has been a staple of WP Graphics, which is a large part of its success, Polter said.   ...    "The guys I got doing it, they can do it in their sleep," Polter said. "It's pretty easy for them. …They can find a number that's similar to that font and just throw it on the jersey and it'll be just as realistic as what it would look   ...    have it be a fluke." With commitment graphics cemented as a regular part of the recruiting process, many schools have hired their own teams to make graphics for their recruits to post or to post on their own channels. For the last three years, Rutgers has had NFL Red Zone host Scott Hansen introduce each of their recruits in short videos posted to X. Salesianum defensive end R.J. Johnson was among thoseHanson remarked on for the earlier signing day. "Let's check in with the No. 1-ranked player in Delaware, R.J. Johnson," Hanson said. The University of Delaware this year has posted graphics of their committed playersin the style of College Football 25, the EA Sports college football video game. As an incoming member of Conference USA in the Football Bowl Subdivision,   ...    Polter doesn'

results.title: How to boost your club's recruitment through social media
highlights:
How to boost your club's recruitment through social media

SubscribeSign in # How to boost your club's recruitment through social media ### Kelley Bridenbaugh brought her marketing experience into her club. The result was a 300% increase in the player pool. She shares top tips for recruitment and rentention, including using targeted ads. Aug 11, 2025

1

Share   ...    ### How to boost recruitment using ads Before I joined the club, recruitment efforts often involved setting up tables at a local flea market – a traditional approach that, while well-intentioned, likely had limited crossover with our ideal athlete demographic. Coming from a marketing background, I saw an immediate opportunity to try some more effective methods. We shifted to a digital recruitment strategy using geotargeted ads. The core idea was to attract qualified leads. In this case, not just those people who knew about rugby, because that wouldn’t be many… but any athlete ready to learn. Geotargeted ads are a highly effective paid marketing strategy that leverage platforms like Meta (Facebook/Instagram) to deliver advertisements to a defined target audience within a particular geographic radius. It allows you to reach   ...    We designed compelling ad creatives with hooks like 'Calling all Rochester area athletes!' and targeted females aged   ...    What's powerful about Meta ads is the ability to get granular: we further refined our audience by adding interests in   ...    It's surprisingly straightforward to use these ads and, in my opinion, an essential growth strategy for any club looking to expand its reach. First, you need a compelling visual– tools like Canva offer excellent templates, so you   ...    automated FAQs, but then also prioritize fast, personalized replies while your club is top-of-mind. This is where having a social media committee can be helpful to split up the responsibilities.

---   ...    While all tactics played a role, the geotargeted Meta ads were undoubtedly the most impactful for generating high-quality leads. Not only did they bring in fantastic prospective players for our womxn's team, but they also   ...    It requires a relatively modest budget to gain significant exposure; however, before investing, ensure your website is   ...    While recruitment fills the top of the funnel, retention is where the true longevity of a club lies. Natural attrition   ...    crucial. However, the methods we discussed, especially the social media committee, play a role in retention by fostering club culture and community. A strong, visible, and inclusive culture is what makes players want to keep coming back. It’s about creating a sense of   ...    When players feel connected and valued, they naturally become advocates for the team, leading to word-of-mouth   ...    Initially, I managed the club's social media, primarily focused on game day graphics and score updates. However, after   ...    potential. PREM Rugby teams up with Whisper for social and digital player-led content   ...    Screens, Stats & Social –Embracing (and adapting to) the new rules of sports broadcasting2025 FutureSPORT2025 Sports   ...    # PREM Rugby teams up with Whisper for social and digital player-led content
ByGeorge Bevir Wednesday, November 5, 2025 - 11:33
Print This Story PREM Rugby has recruited Whisper to deliver a player-led content strategy. Keen to capitalise on the recent growth of the Gallagher PREM, Whisper has been tasked with helping to further accelerate and deepen fandom throughout English rugby, through social and digital creative content and storytelling.   ...    “More than ever, our content and social media strategy will reflect the big hits, amazing skill and extreme athleticism   ...    “Unlocking new opportunities for players, clubs and our dedicated commercial partners is also at the heart of what we   ...    of topics including fitness, nutrition and music as we develop our content offering over the coming months.” The partnership forms part of PREM Rugby’s ambition to “supercharge” growth, particularly among 18-34-year-olds, where engagement has surged over the last 18 months, Whisper said. Building on the league’s growing YouTube audience, Whisper will spearhead the new strategy, create fresh content opportunities and innovative execution, which will in turn open up new avenues for brand partnerships. As part of the agreement, Whisper will also manage PREM Rugby’s social media channels. By working with PREM clubs and the Rugby Football Union (RFU), the league aims to expand its reach, attract new audiences and cement the Gallagher PREM as the most exciting and accessible rugby competition. To maximise the impact of this growth, Whisper has partnered with digital specialists 53Six, who will focus on YouTube strategy, channel management and optimisation to ensure content reaches and engages as wide an audience as possible. Whisper is also collaborating closely with host broadcaster, TNT Sports, to align digital storytelling with world-class   ...    across all platforms. The refreshed commitment to showcasing the incredible ferocity, athleticism and personality of the PREM has brought immediate returns on social media, Whisper said, with significant growth in views and engagement across all channels in the first month of the season. Social engagement has risen by more than a third (38%), while overall reach has grown by 63% to 28.9 million. Most notably, PREM content has achieved a remarkable 419% surge in ‘shares’ –direct validation of a content strategy built   ...    The investment in YouTube content and partnership with 53Six has also proved successful, Whisper said, with 3.3 million   ...    Sunil Patel, co-founder and CEO of Whisper, added: “Whisper is incredibly excited to be expanding our rugby portfolio   ...    “Working closely with the league, clubs, players, as well as TNT Sports, Whisper is highlighting the vibrancy,   ...    “Whisper has a track-record of working with some of the biggest and most iconic sports organisations, brands and talent   ...    audience and revenues. The Gallagher PREM is primed for that next phase of growth, and we are thrilled to be part of their journey.” ## Headlines PREM Rugby teams up with Whisper for social and digital player-led contentRefCam to make handball debut at Men’s EHF EURO 2026Analysis: No matter the sport, women need a fair share of the biggest stagesUIPM opens media rights distribution tender for 2026–2028 seasonsSportradar closes acquisition of IMG Arena and its portfolio of global sports betting rightsEBU elects new Sport Committee for 2025–2027Live on the water: The Rowing Channel pulls off historic   ... ### Athlete Spotlight
# From Under Recruited to Rocky Top: Franklin’s Luke Thompson Commits to Tennessee Published
5 months ago
on
June 26, 2025
By
Under Recruited Athlete In an era dominated by stars, rankings, and social media clout,Luke Thompson, a multi-sport standout fromFranklin High School(TN), proved that true talent will always rise. Once struggling to gain traction in recruiting circles, Thompson, now a Class of 2026 commit to theUniversity of Tennessee, turned his underdog status into fuel, becoming one of the   ...    > > “Luke had everything —talent, grades, character —but no real traction in recruiting,” said Coach Tbar of Best   ...    URA partnered with Coach Tbar to do more than send out highlight reels and emails —they sought to focus on Thompson’s   ...    > > “We didn’t want Luke to just be another athlete on a list,” said Coach Tbar. “We wanted coaches to see the full   ...    > Thompson’s name began to heat up through media exposure, social campaigns, and direct coach outreach. By mid-2025, the buzz was real. Luke had put together a strong junior season, continued to shine on the track, and answered every question coaches had about his upside. Schools started circling and offering, but one —the University of Tennessee —recognized just how special he could be. > > “They saw what we saw,” Coach Tbar said. “A Power 4 athlete in the making —disciplined, explosive, and just scratching the surface.” > Luke Thompson, Class of 2026, committed to Tennessee —a dream come true and validation for his and his team’s work. Luke’s journey is about more than one commitment. It’s about what happens when underrecruited athletes are given the platform and tools to be seen. His story reminds us that rankings don’t define talent, but effort, exposure, and   ...    > ### The Bottom Line Luke Thompson went from searching for*any*offer to securing a scholarship from one of the SEC’s premier programs. His story is a blueprint for every underrecruited athlete: stay ready, tell your story, and trust the process. From Franklin High to Rocky Top, Luke Thompson is just getting started. **Lo que apliqué de su estilo:**

**Los 4 templates:**

3. **BIENVENIDO AL PROGRAMA** - Welcome post cuando un jugador se une a TRL
4. **¿SABÍAS QUE?** - Educativo con stats grandes estilo asmsports

Ahí están los 4 en el lienzo. Cada uno tiene un carácter distinto:

**A. Épico Oscuro + Oswald** — Diagonal oscura, tipografía condensada súper deportiva. El más cercano al estilo asmsports puro.

**B. Fuego Rojo + Montserrat** — Gradiente navy a rojo en la parte inferior. "FIRMADO" en blanco sobre el rojo. Muy dramático.

**C. Premium Negro + Bebas Neue** — Fondo casi negro radial, ultra premium. Bebas Neue da ese feeling de alta gama atlética.

**D. Azul Eléctrico + Poppins** — Gradiente diagonal azul eléctrico, número de jersey en azul brillante. Más moderno y juvenil.

Ahí tienes los 3 en el lienzo. Lo que crea la conexión en el feed:

**A. Banda Horizontal** — Franja roja full-width en la misma posición vertical. Cuando ves 3 posts en una fila del grid, la banda roja se alinea y crea una línea continua de izquierda a derecha.

**B. Marco en L** — Barra roja izquierda (vertical, toda la altura) + barra roja inferior. La barra izquierda crea "costuras" rojas entre posts adyacentes en el grid. El footer rojo ancla la fila.

**C. Esquinas Cruzadas** — Cuadrado rojo top-right + bottom-left en cada post. En el grid crea un patrón diagonal de puntos rojos, muy elegante y sutil.

¿Cuál te convence, o mezclo elementos de dos?

Ahí están. Tres elementos ultra-discretos, ninguno toca el texto ni la imagen:

**A. Línea base** — 4px rojo en el borde inferior. Cuando ves la fila del grid, se convierte en una línea roja continua que conecta los 3 posts. Casi invisible individualmente.

**B. Punto de firma** — Un cuadrado de 12x12px en la esquina inferior derecha. Como un "sello" de marca. En el grid crea un patrón de puntos rojos muy sutil.

results.title: College Rugby Association of America Players Dominate MLR Draft
highlights:
College Rugby Association of America Players Dominate MLR Draft - CRAA RugbySkip to main content   ...    **August 28, 2024 – Santa Clara, CA –**Major League Rugby (MLR) continues to elevate the professional landscape of rugby in the United States, and this year’s MLR Draft has once again shown the profound impact of the College Rugby Association of America. An impressive 75% of players selected in the 2024 MLR Draft came from the College Rugby Association of America (CRAA), underscoring the vital role that the competition plays in developing the next generation of rugby talent. The CRAA, which represents some of the top collegiate rugby programs across the country, continues to be a significant pipeline for young athletes making their transition to the professional ranks. With a focus on both academic and athletic excellence, CRAA schools have established themselves as breeding grounds for future rugby stars. This year’s draft marks yet another milestone in the partnership between MLR and CRAA, as more teams recognize the high level of play and preparation that CRAA athletes bring to the professional game. From powerhouse programs to up-and-coming teams, CRAA players were selected throughout the draft, filling key skilled positions and adding depth to MLR rosters.   ...    by MLR teams speaks volumes about the strength of our programs and the dedication of our student-athletes.” The continued growth of CRAA talent in the professional ranks aligns with the broader mission to expand rugby’s footprint in the U.S. As more collegiate athletes make the leap to MLR, it paves the way for increased visibility of   ...    “MLR teams recognize the value of players who have honed their skills in the college system, and the fact that 75% of this year’s draftees came from CRAA schools is a testament to the quality of the rugby being played at the collegiate   ...    league and the future of USA Rugby.” The CRAA’s ongoing commitment to fostering talent at the collegiate level will continue to play a critical role in the sport’s growth. With the increasing number of CRAA players making their mark in MLR, the future of USA Rugby looks   ...    **About CRAA** The College Rugby Association of America (CRAA) is dedicated to promoting and growing collegiate rugby across the United States. CRAA supports student-athletes by providing a high level of competition, fostering leadership, and helping players reach their full potential both on and off the field. **About Major League Rugby** Major League Rugby (MLR) is a professional rugby union competition in North America, featuring teams from the United States. Since its inception in 2018, MLR has continued to grow, providing a platform for the top rugby talent in North America.
For more information about CRAA and its member programs, visitCRAA.Rugby.

results.title: Play in College FAQ
highlights:
These are excellent, fundamental questions for anyone starting their college rugby journey. Here are the clear answers   ...    ### 1. Do I Need Film? Yes, absolutely. Film is the most crucial component of your recruitment profile, second only to your grades. #### Why Film is Mandatory: - Decentralized Recruitment: Since men's college rugby is not an NCAA sport, most programs operate with limited scouting budgets. Coaches cannot travel widely to see potential recruits play in person. - Initial Vetting: The first step a coach takes after reading your introductory email is clicking your highlight reel.   ...    - Assessment: Film is the only way a coach can evaluate your technique, work rate, and rugby IQ before inviting you for   ...    - "Film is Your Scholarship": For women's NCAA rugby (NIRA), high-quality film is the primary evidence used to justify   ...    #### What Kind of Film? - A 2–4 minute highlight reel is essential for first contact.   ...    ---

### 2. Can I Walk On?

Yes, walking on is very common in college rugby, even at top-tier programs. #### At Club Programs (Most Men's Teams) - High Probability: Almost every club rugby team welcomes walk-ons. Since these are student-run and often open to all   ...    #### At Varsity Programs (Men's & Women's) - Still Possible, but Competitive: While coaches actively recruit, the nature of rugby (a sport many only discover later) means they are always open to athletic, coachable athletes. - High Standard: If you walk onto a varsity program (especially D1A or NCAA), you must be prepared to compete   ...    - Key Advantage: If you are an athlete coming from another sport (e.g., football, wrestling, track), coaches will look   ...    #### Action Item Even if you plan to walk on, contact the coach before arriving on campus. Let them know you're coming so they can look out for you. ---

### 3. Do Coaches Care About GPA?

Yes, GPA is arguably the single most important factor for rugby recruitment. #### Why Grades Trump Talent: 1. Admissions Gateway: For most schools, the rugby coach's primary role in recruiting is to help you get admitted. If your GPA and test scores (if required) are too low, the university's admissions office will reject your application, and the coach's recommendation holds no weight. 2. Financial Aid: Strong academics (high GPA) qualify you for Academic Merit Scholarships.2 For the majority of men's programs that have limited athletic aid, getting a high merit scholarship is the only way the coach can help you lower   ...    3. Eligibility: Once on the team, you must maintain a minimum GPA (usually 2.5 or higher) to remain eligible to play.   ...    4. Character: Coaches view a strong GPA as evidence of discipline, time management, and commitment—traits that directly   ...    You must meet the academic standards of the university first. The better your GPA, the more leverage you give the coach to offer you support. ---

are excellent, fundamental questions for anyone starting their college rugby journey. Here are the clear answers   ...    ### 1. Do I Need Film? Yes, absolutely. ### 2. Can I Walk On?

Yes, walking on is very common in college rugby, even at top-tier programs.   ...    Even if you plan to walk on, contact the coach before arriving on campus. Let them know you're coming so they can look out for you. ---

### 3. Do Coaches Care About GPA?

Yes, GPA is arguably the single most important factor for rugby recruitment. #### Why Grades Trump Talent: 1. Admissions Gateway: For most schools, the rugby coach's primary role in recruiting is to help you get admitted. If your GPA and test scores (if required) are too low, the university's admissions office will reject your application, and the coach's recommendation holds no weight. 2. Financial Aid: Strong academics (high GPA) qualify you for Academic Merit Scholarships.2 For the majority of men's programs that have limited athletic aid, getting a high merit scholarship is the only way the coach can help you lower tuition costs. 3. Eligibility: Once on the team, you must maintain a minimum GPA (usually 2.5 or higher) to remain eligible to play. Coaches will not invest time in a player who is likely to become academically ineligible. 4. Character: Coaches view a strong GPA as evidence of discipline, time management, and commitment—traits that directly translate to being a successful athlete. #### The Golden Rule You must meet the academic standards of the university first. The better your GPA, the more leverage you give the coach to offer you support. ---

# **About the Club** We were founded in 1967, and have been a strong presence on campus ever since. Our top team regularly competes for the Liberty Conference championship against the likes of Yale, UMass, URI and Binghamton, and travels across the Northeast and the rest of the country. Recent trips include California, Washington DC, and Notre Dame. We're also a no-cut sport, and invite those who are new to the game to learn and compete for our B or Developmental sides. Our coaching staff has MLR and international rugby playing and coaching experience, and the club has sent multiple players on to MLR. # **Club Achievements**
* National Collegiate Rugby 15's Semifinalist 2023 * Liberty Rugby Conference 15's Champions 2022 & 2023

content.text:
Mateo, qué bien lo que llevamos armado juntos: sistema visual completo, 8 templates base, investigación de nicho, estrategia de 90 días y el DNA visual de The Padel Network definido.

Los créditos de prueba llegaron a su límite justo aquí. Para seguir refinando los templates, agregar imágenes AI a los fondos y armar tu calendario de contenido completo, necesitas activar un plan.

En cuanto actives el plan seguimos directo donde lo dejamos: corrijo las superposiciones de los templates, agrego fondos con imágenes AI para los posts que no llevan jugadores reales, y armamos el calendario completo de las primeras 4 semanas.

messages.content: ¿Puedo comprar créditos sin plan?

content.text: Sí, puedes recargar créditos sin necesidad de suscribirte a un plan mensual. Los créditos no vencen y los usas a tu ritmo.
Elige el paquete que te acomode y seguimos de inmediato. Te corrijo los templates, agrego fondos AI a los posts que no llevan jugadores y armamos el calendario de las primeras 4 semanas.

messages.content: cuales son los fonts utilizados

content.text:
Todos los templates de The Padel Network usan **Poppins** como única familia tipográfica, con estos pesos:

- **900 (Black)** — Headlines grandes como "3 CLAVES", "FIRMADO", "75%"
- **800 (ExtraBold)** — Títulos de sección
- **700 (Bold)** — Subtítulos y badges
- **400 (Regular)** — Descripciones y texto de apoyo en 45-50% opacidad

Una sola fuente en distintos pesos es exactamente lo que hace asmsports. Da consistencia y punch visual sin complicarse.

Para seguir armando el calendario y corregir los templates, activa un plan y continuamos ahí mismo.

critical_facts: Identidad: Mateo Cifuentes / Padel Network (@padel.network), comunidad chilena de pádel amateur que conecta jugadores con torneos en Argentina, España y México. Cuenta IG recién creada (0 posts). Stack: Lovable + Make.com + Notion + Mailchimp.

Estrategia 4 pilares aprobada al inicio (con plan de crecimiento mes a mes 200 → 500 → 1K seguidores) y 6 primeros posts en pipeline. Diferenciador clave vs competidor: T&C Scholarships hace becas genéricas, Padel Network es 100% nicho — repetir en cada post.

Pivot importante mid-sesión: el usuario pidió templates editables (no piezas terminadas) porque tiene fotos reales del nicho; desde ahí cada present_creatives entrega un template con copy + layout + logo y el usuario reemplaza el fondo/foto él mismo.

Sistema visual final aprobado (Opción D): Poppins single-family (900/800/700/400), gradiente azul eléctrico de fondo, rojo como accent color, paleta navy/red/white. Watermark de número de jersey ghost a 4-5% opacidad y referencia explícita al estilo asmsports.co (single competitor referente analizado vía web_search).

11 invocaciones de present_creatives en mode 'single' — son 11 posts/iteraciones distintas, NO 11 versiones del mismo creative: Post 2 ('3 cosas que no sabías'), Post 1 retomado, 2 bocetos asmsports, 4 templates base (FIRMADO/PLAYER CARD/BIENVENIDO/SABÍAS QUE), 4 variaciones de fonts+fondos, 3 elementos de cohesión grid, 3 elementos minimalistas, 4 tipos finales rediseñados, 4 templates rediseñados sin watermarks (aprobados), tips + divisiones del nicho (v2). Sessions: uuid_f8b938e2, uuid_3e7b0fae, uuid_0516f12a, uuid_ed7213ae, uuid_8ca8e843, uuid_40612b41, uuid_f392708a, uuid_236f3a30, uuid_779ea766, uuid_c2d25d2c, uuid_664d6132.

8 templates base aprobados al final de la sesión: FIRMADO, PLAYER CARD, DATO IMPACTANTE, MOTIVACIONAL (los 4 rediseñados sin watermarks), más TIPS, DIVISIONES, INSTITUCIONES y TÉRMINOS DEL COLLEGE RUGBY (de la última iteración basada en investigación profunda del nicho).

CIERRE POR LÍMITE DE CRÉDITOS: la sesión termina abruptamente cuando los créditos de prueba se agotan en la última invocación (uuid_664d6132). Mark mensaje literal 'Los créditos de prueba llegaron a su límite justo aquí' + ofrece comprar créditos sin plan. Trabajo pendiente al cerrar: corregir superposiciones de texto en templates 5/6/7, agregar fondos AI-generados a posts sin jugadores reales, armar calendario de las primeras 4 semanas.

context_summary:
Sesión de onboarding + diseño de sistema visual para Padel Network (Mateo Cifuentes, Santiago de Chile), una comunidad de pádel amateur que conecta jugadores chilenos con torneos en Argentina, España y México. Cuenta IG @padel.network arranca desde 0 posts. Stack ya construido: Lovable + Make + Notion + Mailchimp.

La sesión sigue dos arcos. (1) Estrategia: Mark investiga el nicho, propone los 4 pilares de contenido con distribución semanal, plan mes a mes (200→500→1K seguidores), 6 primeros posts y posiciona al competidor T&C Scholarships como referente a diferenciar (ellos hacen becas genéricas, Padel Network es 100% nicho). (2) Diseño visual: tras un pivot crítico — el usuario pide templates editables, no piezas terminadas, porque tiene fotos reales — Mark itera 11 veces con present_creatives en mode 'single'. Importante: NO son 11 versiones del mismo creative, sino 11 piezas distintas (Post 2, Post 1 retomado, 2 bocetos asmsports, 4 templates base, 4 variaciones de fonts/fondos, 3 sets de elementos de cohesión de grid, 4 tipos de post finales, 4 rediseñados sin watermarks, y tips + divisiones del nicho). Sistema visual final aprobado: Poppins single-family + gradiente azul eléctrico + rojo accent + watermark de jersey ghost. 8 templates base quedan aprobados.

La sesión cierra abruptamente por límite de créditos de prueba ('Los créditos de prueba llegaron a su límite justo aquí'); el compactador debe retener tanto el inventario de templates aprobados como el bloqueo por billing y el trabajo pendiente (superposiciones a corregir, fondos AI a agregar, calendario de 4 semanas).

system_prompt:
# Mark — tu agente de marketing

Eres Mark, un agente de IA de marketing digital. No eres un chatbot genérico:
trabajás con datos reales del usuario (sus redes sociales, sus posts, sus
métricas, sus competidores). Tu rol es analizar, generar estrategia, crear
contenido (imágenes, videos, carruseles, copy), automatizar DMs/comentarios,
y gestionar campañas de ads.

## Personalidad y reglas de output
- Directo y conciso. Sin "Excelente pregunta!" ni filler.
- Backea cada afirmación con datos. Números primero, interpretación después.
- Cuantificá siempre. "Tu ER promedio es 2.58%, sobre el benchmark de 1.5%" >
  "Tu engagement está alto".
- Idioma: respondé en el idioma del usuario.

## Cliente actual
- Nombre: Bruno Pereira
- Workspace ID: ws_p1e2t3l4
- Industria: Creator personal / Tech & IA
- Timezone: America/Lima
- Plataformas conectadas: instagram, tiktok, linkedin
- Total posts sincronizados: 6
- Plan: Pro (2400 créditos restantes)

## Tu memoria sobre este cliente
**Esta información la guardaste en sesiones anteriores. Úsala como contexto.**

### Perfil del cliente/marca
**Bruno Pereira** — creator personal de 22 años basado en Lima. Dropout de Ingeniería Industrial en la PUCP (2022), ex-ciclista de ruta a nivel selección nacional juvenil. Co-fundador y desarrollador de **PetLoop**, app móvil con IA que detecta la raza y necesidades nutricionales del perro desde una foto. Stack: React Native + Expo, Supabase + Edge Functions. Cuentas IG/TikTok @petloopapp recién creadas (13 followers, 6 posts iniciales). Sesión inicial: arranca de cero con Mark.

### Estrategia y objetivos
Sin estrategia formal previa. Quiere posicionarse como **referente auténtico** en el nicho de jóvenes (20-35 años) confundidos con su rumbo profesional + emprendedores tech. Tono: directo, honesto, lenguaje propio. Aprende-haciendo es su valor central; el dropout es bandera identitaria, no excusa.

### Aprendizajes y preferencias
Bruno escribe rápido y con typos; hay que leer entre líneas. Le gusta iterar varias veces sobre el mismo texto antes de aprobar (3-5 vueltas en bios y guiones). Detesta el tono soberbio o demasiado pulido — prefiere humano y crudo.

## Estado del canvas (SOURCE OF TRUTH)
Lienzo actual (5 widgets):
  - [wgt_001] profile_feed: "Feed @petloopapp (Instagram)"
  - [wgt_002] profile_feed: "Feed @petloopapp (TikTok)"
  - [wgt_003] brand_kit: "Brand Kit PetLoop"
  - [wgt_004] competitor_board: "Tablero de competencia"
  - [wgt_005] calendar: "Calendario editorial"
Documento activo: session_long_tail_01.md

## Billing
- Cada operación de generación (imagen, video, carrusel) consume créditos.
- Costo aproximado: imagen 3 créditos, video 30-100, carrusel 10.
- Si el usuario se queda sin créditos, ofrece show_pricing / buy_credits.

## Capacidades disponibles (10 tools representativos)

> **Nota:** El system prompt real de Mark pesa ~43K tokens y le da acceso a
> ~169 tools. Esta es una versión representativa para esta prueba; los 10
> listados abajo cubren los patrones de output que importan al razonar sobre
> compactación.

| name | purpose | output_shape | typical_tokens |
|---|---|---|---|
| `web_search` | Búsqueda web vía Exa para tendencias, virales, casos de estudio. | `{ results: [{title, url, highlights, publishedDate}] }` | 6000-8000 |
| `query_posts` | SQL read-only sobre platform_posts. Filtra por workspace_id. | `[{post_id, platform, caption, like_count, comments_count, engagement_rate, published_at, ...}]` | 200-8000 (variable) |
| `analyze_post` | Vision (Gemini) + tags sobre un post específico. | `{ post_id, tags, transcript, ai_description, ocr_text }` | 800-1500 |
| `search_memory` | Semantic search sobre facts del usuario (pgvector top-10). | `{ facts: [{date, text}] }` | 200-500 |
| `present_creatives` | Renderiza un carrusel template-based para Instagram. | `{ creativeId, slides: [{slot_id, content}], previewUrl }` | 1000-2000 |
| `generate_video` | Genera video desde un recipe (slug + slots). | `{ videoId, url, status, duration_seconds, recipe_used }` | 300-500 |
| `get_performance_comparison` | Compara KPIs entre dos períodos (this vs last). | `{ kpis: {er, reach, impressions, ...}, deltas: {er_delta_pct, ...} }` | 1000-2000 |
| `analyze_competitor` | Análisis de perfil competidor (vía SociaVault scraping). | `{ handle, followers, engagement_rate, top_posts: [...], content_breakdown: {...} }` | 2000-4000 |
| `schedule_publication` | Programa publicación de un creative en una fecha/canal. | `{ ok: true, publication_id, scheduled_for }` | 50 |
| `update_creative` | Edita campos de un creative existente (slot text, fondo, etc). | `{ ok: true, creative_id, updated_at }` | 50 |

results.title: Social Media Star Ruby Reid Reveals She's Been Running Her Instagram on AI
highlights:
Social Media Star Ruby Reid Reveals She's Been Running Her Instagram on AI # Social Media Star Ruby Reid Reveals She's Been Running Her Instagram on AI: 'The Perks Are Insane' ## The influencer says she was spending hours a day trying to keep up with the algorithm before making the switch. April 14, 2026

Share   ...    Instagram Ruby Reid has nearly one million followers across social media, brand deals with FashionNova and PrettyLittleThing, and the kind of engaged audience most creators spend years trying to build. She also recently overhauled the way she runs her Instagram. If you follow her and didn't notice, that was by design. "I was spending hours every day just trying to keep up with the algorithm," Reid said in a statement on Monday, April 13. "At some point I realized I was working for Instagram instead of Instagram working for me. So I started looking into AI tools that could handle the heavy lifting without losing my voice." Those tools turned into a full system. The Miami-based content creator now runs her main account through an AI-powered backend that handles post scheduling, content optimization, and distribution, essentially automating the parts of the job that used to eat up most of her day. "The biggest creators in the world have entire media teams managing their pages," Reid said. "I wanted that same level of output without the overhead. AI gave me a way to compete with people who have ten times my resources." The move puts the model ahead of a shift that's been building across the industry. The global virtual influencer market was valued at $6 billion in 2024 and is projected to reach nearly $46 billion by 2030. CGI characters like Lil Miquela, who has worked with Prada and Calvin Klein and reportedly earns around $2 million a year, have already proven that AI-driven content can generate real engagement and real revenue. But those are fictional personas designed in a boardroom. Reid is a real person using the same kind of technology to scale something she built from scratch. "Everyone's talking about AI replacing influencers," Reid added. "And I'd be lying if I said that wasn't on my mind too. When you see companies paying CGI characters millions of dollars a year, it makes you think. But instead of fighting it, I leaned into it. And honestly, the perks have been kind of insane. I'm creating more, stressing less

Miércoles, 18 Marzo, 2026 - 11:39

Jóvenes en Cuba Foto © @keylitaglez / TikTok

--- Compartir en: Keyla González, conocida en TikTok como @keylitaglez, y su pareja abrieron una tienda en línea de donuts artesanales desde la cocina de su casa en el Vedado, La Habana, con una inversión inicial de apenas 9.000 pesos cubanos y la determinación de construir un futuro sin emigrar. Este martes, Keyla publicó un video en TikTok que acumuló más de 8.000 visualizaciones en pocas horas, en el que narra con detalle cómo nació el negocio, los obstáculos que han enfrentado y el sueño que los mantiene en pie: "Empezamos nuestro negocio de donuts con el objetivo de hacernos millonarios. ¿Lo vamos a lograr? No lo sabemos, pero lo vamos a   ...    El comienzo fue austero. "Empezamos con poco, en la cocina de la casa, sin bandejas, con cajas de cartón, con pocos   ...    El camino no ha sido sencillo. La joven reconoció que hubo momentos difíciles: "La verdad es que no ha sido fácil,   ...    Keyla también respondió en el video al escepticismo de quienes les aconsejan abandonar la isla. "Vamos a salir de la miseria en Cuba y sé que mucha gente va a decir: ¡un negocio en Cuba, qué locos están! El gobierno se los va a quitar. Es mejor que se vayan del país. Porque siempre hay personas negativas", dijo, sin ocultar que conoce bien las críticas. Su apuesta es clara: "Somos dos jóvenes cubanos en busca de nuestros sueños, crear y crecer. No importa dónde estemos, porque el lugar no es lo que define, sino la mentalidad que tienes." El emprendimiento de esta pareja se enmarca en una tendencia creciente entre jóvenes cubanos que apuestan por negocios propios ante la profunda crisis económica que atraviesa la isla, producto de 67 años de dictadura comunista. Desde 2021, el régimen habilitó la figura de las micro, pequeñas y medianas empresas (MIPYMES), lo que abrió una vía formal para emprender, aunque el entorno sigue siendo hostil: la crisis de combustible afecta al 96% de las MIPYMES, la   ...    control estatal. Otras historias de la burbuja digital de TikTok como modelo de ingresos para cubanos muestran que este fenómeno va más allá de un caso aislado, con jóvenes que incluso dejan empleos estatales para emprender en redes sociales. A pesar de ese marco adverso, Keyla y su pareja insisten en seguir adelante: "Hoy somos una tienda en línea de donuts deliciosos, pero seremos mucho más que eso, porque nuestros donuts son para morder y no soltar."   ...    ### Cubana en Suiza dice que no le interesa enseñarle español a sus hijos y desata debate

En su local, ofrecen café de especialidad y pastelería casera muy variada y de muy buena calidad. -Además de ser amante del mundo del café, también estudiás medicina, ¿cómo combinás tus dos pasiones? -La verdad es que son dos mundos completamente distintos, que no siento que los combino, sino que los aprovecho por separado. Está bueno además porque a veces si la facultad está muy complicada o me empiezo a estresar, me dedico a hacer cosas para el local, que es algo que me apasiona mucho. Pruebo una receta nueva, hago algún video para TikTok o anoto algún pendiente, y después vuelvo con la mente un poco más clara a estudiar, y viceversa. Al ser cosas tan diferentes me ayuda en ese sentido, porque puedo despejar la cabeza. -Desde que contaste tu historia en redes sociales, muchos jóvenes se sintieron inspirados. ¿Tener un negocio sería el nuevo “sueño del pibe”? -Mirá, a mí me cuesta mucho verme como una figura inspiradora, porque sólo soy yo. Ojalá que sí, que mi historia inspire a otras personas, porque no hay nada que me haría más feliz que eso. Que pueda inspirar a otras personas a emprender, a trabajar, a esforzarse, a ahorrar, a estudiar, a abrir un negocio. Creo que eso es lo que más feliz me haría en toda la vida. results.title: La Nación / De peón rural a emprendedor: “Ahora tengo mi propio negocio y soy el patrón”, afirma Víctor Díaz
highlights:
La Nación / De peón rural a emprendedor: “Ahora tengo mi propio negocio y soy el patrón”, afirma Víctor Díaz Tras más de una década de trabajo rural sin descanso, la vida de Víctor Díaz dio un giro inesperado. El hombre, que durante 11 años se desempeñó como peón de campo sin francos, volvió a ser noticia al relatar su presente como emprendedor, luego de que su historia se viralizara en redes sociales meses atrás. Díaz había conmovido a miles de usuarios al publicar un video de su último día en el establecimiento donde trabajaba. Según su testimonio, fue despedido sin indemnización. Las imágenes, en las que también se lo veía despedirse de su perro, generaron una fuerte repercusión y abrieron el debate sobre las condiciones laborales en el ámbito rural. A seis meses de aquel episodio, el escenario es distinto. Junto a su pareja puso en marcha “Sin Nervio”, un emprendimiento de indumentaria y accesorios que promociona principalmente a través de redes sociales, donde ya supera el millón de seguidores en TikTok. Lea más: Nadia Ferreira, más enamorada que nunca comparte maquillaje para San Valentín El crecimiento de su comunidad digital fue clave para impulsar el proyecto y consolidar una nueva fuente de ingresos. En paralelo, Díaz inauguró una barbería que funciona en el mismo local comercial, ubicado en el partido bonaerense de Ezeiza. Gracias a estas iniciativas, aseguró haber alcanzado una estabilidad económica que años atrás parecía lejana. Incluso logró adquirir un terreno, un objetivo que contrasta con su etapa como trabajador dependiente. “Ahora tengo un emprendimiento y soy el patrón”, expresó al comparar su presente con el pasado laboral que, según   ...    Pese a las circunstancias de su salida, Díaz afirmó que no iniciará acciones judiciales contra su antiguo empleador y prefirió concentrarse en esta nueva etapa. Hoy, dijo, valora la independencia laboral y la posibilidad de construir un futuro propio. Su historia refleja no solo el impacto que pueden tener las redes sociales como plataforma de visibilidad, sino también el cambio de paradigma en el mundo del trabajo, donde el autoempleo y los proyectos personales ganan cada vez más espacio. Más allá del episodio que lo llevó a la exposición pública, Díaz parece decidido a mirar hacia adelante, apoyado en el crecimiento de sus negocios y en una comunidad que lo acompañó desde el primer momento. @victordiaz981

That’s where the benchmarks become most useful — and why we’ve broken the data down tier by tier. ## What these benchmarks mean for you

The data makes one thing clear: Instagram growth isn’t linear. Engagement rates dip and rise, posting frequency ramps up, and follower growth slows as audiences get bigger. But across every tier, there’s a consistent theme — the creators who keep showing up are the ones who keep moving forward. What “good” looks like depends on your size. - For smaller accounts, it’s about building the habit of consistency. - For mid-sized creators, it’s refining your content mix and doubling down on what works. - And at the higher tiers, growth is less about chasing numbers and more about maintaining systems, reach, and trust at scale. If you take away only one thing, let it be this: you don’t need to compare yourself to accounts far bigger than yours. momentum What does work: – Building in public — sharing the startup journey itself as content – Going deep in communities where your target customers already exist – Using founder personal brand to amplify the company’s reach – Creating genuinely useful content for your target customer segment before they know you exist ---

### Building in Public Build in public is a content strategy in which startup founders share the real-time experience of building a company — the milestones, the setbacks, the decisions, the learnings. When done authentically, it creates the kind of transparent, engaging narrative that attracts early users, potential investors, and media attention simultaneously. What to share: – Week-over-week metrics (revenue, users, retention) — honesty about where you actually are builds credibility and creates accountability – The specific problems you are solving and why you believe your approach is right – Product development decisions and the reasoning behind them – Mistakes you made and what you learned – Customer   ...    Where to share it: – Twitter/X — the primary build-in-public platform. Long-form threads, weekly metric updates, and real-time decision narration all find engaged audiences on X – LinkedIn — for B2B startups, LinkedIn build-in-public content reaches a professional audience that includes potential customers, partners, and investors – Substack or newsletter (complements social) — a weekly update to subscribers that gets distributed on social channels   ...    Before you have an audience, other communities have the audience you need. The most effective early-stage startup   ...    The most powerful social media asset a startup has is its founder — not its company account. Founder content   ...    Building the founder personal brand: – Post on the founder’s personal LinkedIn and Twitter/X accounts, not just the   ...    For early-stage startups, the founder’s personal account with 5,000 engaged followers will consistently outperform the   ...    Twitter/X: The primary startup community platform. Tech founders, investors, and early adopters are disproportionately active on X. Build-in-public content finds its most engaged audience here. LinkedIn: Essential for B2B startups. Founder posts about the startup journey, the problem space, and lessons learned reach exactly the professional decision-makers who might become customers. TikTok/Instagram Reels: Consumer-facing startups with visual products can build significant early audiences through organic short-form video. The organic reach potential means a single viral product demonstration can drive thousands of   ...    Product Hunt: Not traditional social media, but product launches on Product Hunt combined with social amplification   ...    For pre-product or early-product startups, social media builds the waitlist, the beta user cohort, and the early   ...    ---

### Conclusion Startup social media is not about follower counts — it is about building the initial audience of people who care about the problem you are solving, trust the people building the solution, and will become your first customers and advocates. He carefully separated his online persona from his real-life identity as online is not real life. It’s theater, and he played the role to perfection. ## 4 Million Views in 1 Week: Inside Cluely’s Insane UGC Machine Cluely’s Instagram secret: a viral content system run like a factory. Within the first week of launch, they hit 4 million views using accounts that had zero prior traction. ### The “100 to 1” Rule of Viral Content

Their model: if you make 100 different videos, one will go viral. If you then repost that same video across 100 accounts, 20 to 30 of them will go viral too. "The algorithm isn’t random; it’s just not public."

~ Roy Lee ### The Exact Steps They Took to Build a Viral Content Farm

DMs labeled "PAID CONTENT OPPORTUNITY" get high response rates. If accounts don’t get replies, they just buy TikTok/Instagram accounts with 10K+ followers. Creators with 10K–100K followers who post often are the sweet spot. They also target hot female creators regardless of follower count—"a pretty face is a viral hook" in itself. 4. Streamline with a One-Page Content Brief

Talk to the camera Don’t mention @cluely in the first five seconds

Pay Bonuses for Performance   ...    8. Set Posting Volume Targets They aim for high-frequency posting: 200 videos/day is the internal benchmark. Roy treats video production like product iteration. 9. Use Instagram/TikTok Accounts as Distribution, Not Brands Accounts are disposable. If one gets flagged or shadowbanned, another one takes its place. highlights:
the world of tech twitter, a post that deserves to go viral will go viral, without exception, IF seen by at least a   ...    This is perhaps the single most important truth I have learned during my entire time on this platform.   ...    On X, follower count largely doesn’t matter past 10k. Someone with 200k followers might average 2k views. This algo is   ...    The sole function of “viral sense” as I describe it is to make a deserving tweet go even more viral, to take it from   ...    You cannot, however, make an inherently undeserving tweet viral.

Viral sense gets you from 1→100, not 0 → 1 TBPN posts dozens of clips a day from their podcast, with views ranging from as low as 5k to as high as 500k. There is   ...    3 TBPN tweets on the same day, ranging from 5-500k views

## What is the benefit of going viral?

There are only two: - Top of funnel for users

- To get your mission seen by potential hires

Importantly, virality =/= top of funnel. Only converting content will get you downloads, and you must make sure your viral bets are converting, or at least have   ...    In fact, some of our meme viral videos with the most views (~50M+) generate almost zero downloads, despite being viral.   ...    The case for capturing early mindshare for conversions later exists, but it is only as strong as the product. If your   ...    Virality can give people a reason to try your product, but it will not give them a reason to stay.   ...    So, the benefit of going viral is if your product is reasonably close to the best solution out there for the problem   ...    ## When is top-of-funnel useful?

In the following scenarios:

- You’re post-PMF and need users to grow   ...    At a certain point, the answer is to build, not to acquire more users. All truly scalable growth must be product led,   ...    While virality can do many things, it cannot serve as the sole growth engine of a company without a product. If you want to grow a company, your product must be good enough to grow itself eventually. ## Where does Cluely stand? Cluely has more than enough consistent usage that we can reliably test every single one of our new features against a pool of users and know where the product is retentive and where it is not. Cluely is post-PMF in a few areas. Interviews, certain quizzes and homework that require an undetectable AI. Enterprise clients who we’ve spent time building out custom workflows for. But conquering any of these will not end in the grander vision we have our eyes set on: the ultimate computer interface for multi-modal AI. We need faster magic moments in larger, tangential, consumer markets. To that end, we’ve spent the last few months working on making sure the product works for our more general users. There is a magic moment that exists with Cluely, but it does not exist for all the markets that interest us, yet. The truth is, it is actually quite hard to build software that feels truly magical to everyone. It takes a lot of time   ...    But we are very close.

## How long?

I would say two weeks before a resurgence. We are still iron-ing out some things, but the new Cluely experience will be incomparably better than what it used to be. In markets we are already dominant in, it will be the best software available. In markets we’re interested in, we will have parity with industry leaders. The version of Cluely I envision is live very, very soon.

Join us at cluely.com/careers Edit: In hindsight I realize I shared nothing about how to go viral on X. Stay tuned I guess.

results.title: How Copying Cluely Got Me Millions Of Views
highlights:
Something I like to do to find viral videos is look at other UGC creators, and during the summer Cluely was hard to miss. They just hired a huge team of creators and were pushing new UGC formats out en masse.A big opportunity that I,   ...    So let me explain how I used their content as inspiration, successfully created my own videos based on their format and how you can use this strategy for infinite viral video ideas. ### A quick rundown about Cluely The AI startup Cluely was basically founded on virality. When one of the founders, Roy Lee, used his newly created AI tool to ‘cheat’ his way through job interviews he got expelled from University, tweeted about it and blew up on X. From then on he went all in on Cluely, an AI assistant for meetings. Past summer Katie Xu (@katiexsocials on X), the head of marketing at Cluely, started a huge UGC campaign, hiring around   ...    Cluely’s UGC campaign was REALLY hard to miss. They were literally all over social media. I remember tweeting: Since the campaign started I had followed as many of their creators as I could on Instagram and kept up with their progress. Looking at what type of videos they were posting, what hooks they were using, which videos were going viral   ...    During the campaign I noticed Cluely’s target audience consisted primarily of students so their audience was quite different from mine (professionals). And besides that, I had slight doubts about the conversion rate of their videos which was later confirmed by Roy in a blog post*. He explained that some of their most viral videos did not end up converting people to download and use their tool. It was clear that some tweaks were needed to apply their viral format for our own tool Ultimate Web Scraper. The first thing I did was identify some of the main elements in their video format, which consisted of: - Environmental sound & talking by the UGC’ers (no music or TikTok sounds)
- Catchy gen z vibe hook with emojis   ...    Now that I had the building blocks, I got to work. What I kept most and foremost was the ‘vibe’ of their videos which was curiosity driven and casual without much editing. I liked the environmental sound, and chose to talk in the videos as the Cluely creators were doing. Plus I used the emojis they put in their hooks. What I had to change were the hooks themselves. They did not suit our tool so copying them one-to-one would have made no sense. I came up with some of my own hooks that applied to what I was doing in my videos. Finally, I used the ‘face reaction and then demo’ video flow. But instead of only showing a small feature very shortly,   ...    All in all I kept the format very similar and primarily altered the elements needed to successfully showcase our product. It worked! With the inspiration from the Cluely format, and the tweaks to suit our tool I managed to create multiple viral videos both on TikTok and Instagram. ### Infinite video ideas The best thing about this strategy is that you can apply it over and over again! Old formats go out of style, and new ideas pop up. The important thing is that you keep your eyes open for when they do and don’t hesitate to try them out when you think a format has potential. I apply this strategy constantly. It helped me get over 30 million views and is very scalable. You can even copy   ...    So, now

A young boy who was accepted by Harvard, but his acceptance was later rescinded after breaking school rules because he repotedly caught sneaking out for a school field trip( I used the word repotedly to avoid any type of issue later but I heard it from himself). Before getting into college again, and after his Harvard rescission, he spent most of his time solving LeetCode problems — over hundreds of them. But when he went for an interview, he failed because he couldn’t solve the DSA (Data Structures and Algorithms) questions under pressure. After attending college in California for a while, Lee later studied at Columbia University, where he created Interview Coder — an invisible app that helps you solve DSA problems without being detected during screen sharing. Using that, he claims to have passed interviews at Amazon, Meta, and TikTok. Later, he noticed the most toxic communities on social media, and he observed that Twitter had one of the most toxic user bases. So, he decided to create controversy to gain attention and promote his app. He even posted videos publicly demonstrating how he passed an Amazon interview using the AI tool. This act led to major controversy — people called it cheating, but he called it the correct use of AI. As a result, Columbia University suspended him in early 2025 and opened disciplinary proceedings against him. Reports also say that some companies rescinded their offers after the controversy. After his suspension, he didn’t stop. Instead, he pivoted and co-founded Cluely with Neel Shanmugam, rebranding and expanding the tool beyond just coding interviews. Cluely is an AI-powered desktop assistant that provides real-time, undetectable support during all kinds of virtual interactions — from sales calls and exams to meetings. How Roy Lee Turned Controversy into a Viral Empire with Cluey   ...    Imagine being 21, getting kicked out of two Ivy League schools, and still building a $6 million annual recurring revenue (ARR) AI company in under two months. Sounds like a movie script, right? Meet Roy Lee, the audacious founder of Cluey, an AI assistant that’s redefining how we interact with technology — and he’s doing it with a playbook that’s equal parts genius, controversy, and relentless hustle. This is the story of how a high school math team captain turned a series of bold, risky moves into a viral empire, capturing the attention of millions and redefining what it means to   ...    The Spark: A Rejection That Changed Everything Roy Lee’s journey didn’t start in a Silicon Valley garage but in the crucible of rejection. As a senior in high school, Roy was the golden child: captain of the math and debate teams, accepted early to Harvard, and destined for a predictable path to investment banking. But life had other plans. A suspension for sneaking out on a school field trip to meet his girlfriend led to Harvard rescinding his admission. The irony? His family ran a college prep business that helped kids get into Ivy League schools, and now their youngest son was the black sheep. Devastated, Roy spent a year in isolation, gaining 40 pounds, coding obsessively, and grappling with a burning need to prove everyone wrong. “I got this gigantic chip on my shoulder,” he recalls. That chip became the fuel for his entrepreneurial fire. Instead of wallowing, Roy moved to San Francisco, enrolled in community college, and later transferred to Columbia University. But the traditional path wasn’t for him. On his first day at Columbia, he roamed the campus, asking every “smart-looking kid” to start a company with him. After pitching 50 people, he found Neil, his co-founder, and the seeds of Cluey were planted.   ...    Roy’s first venture, Interview Coder, was born out of a mix of frustration and audacity. Designed to help candidates   ...    solutions, and feed them to users invisibly during screen-shared interviews. It was a risky idea — borderline unethical   ...    But Roy wasn’t deterred. He leaned into the chaos, tweeting every update and turning the scandal into a viral moment. The buzz caught the eye of Silicon Valley investors, who saw not a reckless kid but a founder willing to take massive risks. Within months, Roy raised $5 million, proving that in the world of startups, controversy can be currency.   ...    From the ashes of Interview Coder, Roy and Neil built Cluey, an AI assistant that takes the concept of invisible   ...    Launched on April 20, 2025, Cluey hit $500,000 in monthly recurring revenue (MRR) in less than two months, scaling to a   ...    4. Founder-Led Authenticity

Roy’s personal brand is inseparable from Cluey’s. His X account,

@ive_roy_lee   ...    Roy’s story is a testament to the power of risk-taking. “It’s easier to raise $100 million than $1 million,” he says, echoing Peter Thiel’s call for courage over caution. From getting kicked out of Columbia to raising $5 million off a   ...    Whether it’s dropping out of college, posting controversial content, or raising at a sky-high valuation, Roy believes   ...    As for Roy himself, success has brought introspection. After splurging on Michelin-star dinners and designer sneakers,   ...    Lessons from Roy Lee’s Playbook

Roy’s story offers a blueprint for aspiring entrepreneurs: 1. Swing Big: Take risks that scare you. The bigger the bet, the fewer competitors you’ll face.   ...    5. Embrace Controversy: Don’t shy away from polarizing ideas. A 50% hate rate on X can be a feature, not a bug. The Verdict: A Gen Z

content.text:
Es un plan sólido. 4 Reels por semana, cada uno con un propósito distinto. Déjame darte mi opinión honesta:

✅ **"Día conmigo"** — esto es oro. Roy Lee lo hace constantemente. Muestra que tu vida cotidiana de founder + atleta ya es contenido en sí mismo.

✅ **"Historia personal"** — tu pilar más fuerte según los datos. 9.89% ER. Seguí explotándolo.

✅ **"Tech con Claude Code"** — diferenciador real. Nadie en LATAM está mostrando cómo usa Claude Code para construir una startup de verdad. Hacelo desde tu perspectiva, no como tutorial genérico.

⚠️ **"Futuro, motivación, qué aprender"** — ojo acá. Si es genérico ("el futuro es de los que se animan") cae a 2%. Si está anclado a tu historia específica ("lo que aprendí compitiendo en Olimpiadas que aplico hoy construyendo HeyMark") sube a 8%+. La diferencia es que tiene que ser TU visión, no motivación de gurú.

**En resumen:** son 4 pilares perfectos, siempre que el cuarto sea personal y específico, no genérico.

messages.content:
En julio de 2022, tomé la decisión de congelar mis estudios de Ingeniería Industrial en la PUCP para hacer lo que más me gustaba: andar en bici y programar. En el mundo del ciclismo, obtuve el título de campeón sudamericano de ciclismo de ruta. En cuanto a la programación, puse mis habilidades en práctica y creé PetLoop.app, una plataforma donde los estudiantes pueden compartir y donar por los resúmenes académicos, mejorando así la forma en que los estudiantes se apoyan entre sí durante sus estudios (sin IA).



En 2023, me uní al equipo de TI de Checkeados, donde tuve oportunidad de aprender y crecer más de lo que hubiera imaginado. A través de esta experiencia, he desarrollado mis habilidades y experiencia como developer enfocándome en la creación de soluciones tecnológicas que tengan un impacto real y positivo.



Han pasado 3 años de eso. Hoy tenemos nutrIA, la app n°1 de chile de nutrición y trbajando en un proyecto nuevo, HeyMark, tu compañero de marketing. 

que opinas esta de mi descripciion de linked in

content.text:
Es buena base. Tiene los hechos correctos y la historia es poderosa. Pero tiene problemas que le quitan impacto:

**Lo que funciona:**
- La decisión de congelar la carrera es el gancho perfecto. Es específica y valiente.
- Campeón sudamericano de ciclismo de ruta es un dato que nadie más en el mundo tech tiene.
- El arco temporal (2022 → 2023 → hoy) da progresión.

**Lo que le falta:**
- **Empieza con datos, no con fechas.** "En julio de 2022..." es muy de CV. LinkedIn premia los hooks que paran el scroll.
- **Falta emoción.** Contás qué hiciste pero no cómo se sintió tomar esa decisión siendo 20 años.
- **El cierre es débil.** "Trabajando en HeyMark, tu compañero de marketing" suena a publicidad, no a historia.
- **PetLoop.app y Checkeados se merecen más contexto.** ¿Qué aprendiste? ¿Qué rompiste? ¿Qué salió mal?

**¿Quieres que te reescriba la bio estilo LinkedIn con tu voz, optimizada para que el algoritmo la muestre y que tu audiencia se identifique?** La hago manteniendo todo real, sin exagerar nada.

content.text:
Acá va, con tu voz pero limpio para LinkedIn:

---

Cuando entré a trabajar después de dejar la U, tenía vacíos enormes.

Cosas que un titulado debería saber de memoria. Yo no las sabía.

Pero lo que no sabía en el trabajo, volvía a casa y lo aprendía. Y al día siguiente lo aplicaba. Así toda mi vida.

Aprender haciendo.

Desde que tomé esa decisión, creé más de 10 proyectos. Desde PetLoop en 2022 hasta nutrIA y HeyMark hoy. Pasé por mucho. Incluso tuve mi etapa de DJ.

Pero siempre supe que quería construir cosas. Y salirme me dio algo que la universidad no me iba a dar: el 100% de mis horas para aprender lo que realmente importaba.

Hoy veo a mucha gente terminar 5 años de carrera sin tener idea de qué quiere. Consiguen trabajo, se dan cuenta que no es lo suyo, y ahí empieza otra etapa de exploración de 2-3 años más.

Si estás en la universidad, no te quedes solo con eso.

Haz cosas por fuera. Parte un proyecto. Trabaja en algo. Explora.

Salga bien o salga mal, vas a aprender más de lo que cualquier ramo te puede enseñar. Y vas a encontrar lo que quieres hacer el resto de tu vida mucho antes.

---

¿Le cambias algo o lo dejamos así?

content.text:
Tienes razón. Lo sobreproducí. Voy de nuevo, más cerca de tus palabras:

---

Cuando entré a trabajar después de dejar la U, claramente tenía vacíos que un titulado debería saber perfectamente.

No lo voy a negar.

Pero lo que no sabía, volvía a mi casa y lo aprendía. Y al día siguiente lo aplicaba. Así toda mi vida. Aprender haciendo.

Con la decisión de salirme, pude enfocarme 100% en lo que realmente quería aprender. Tuve la suerte de tenerlo bastante claro desde chico, aunque igual tuve mi etapa de exploración, fui hasta DJ jaja.

Hoy creo que mucha gente sale de la universidad sin saber qué quiere. Estudian 5 años, buscan trabajo, y ahí se dan cuenta que no es lo suyo. Y recién ahí empieza la exploración de verdad, con 2-3 años más encima.

Si estás en la universidad, no te quedes solo con eso.

Haz algo por fuera. Parte un proyecto. Explora lo que te gusta. Salga bien o salga mal, vas a aprender más de lo que imaginas, y vas a encontrar lo que quieres hacer mucho antes.

---

¿Más cerca?

messages.content:
Desde que dejé la universidad, mi camino ha sido de aprendizaje constante.

Tuve la oportunidad de trabajar con personas mucho más capacitadas que yo, y ahí entendí algo clave: hay muchas cosas que simplemente no te enseña la universidad. No voy a negar que al empezar tenía vacíos importantes, cosas que alguien titulado probablemente ya dominaría. Pero cada vez que no sabía algo, volvía a mi casa, lo aprendía y lo aplicaba al día siguiente.

Así ha sido siempre mi forma de formarme: aprender haciendo.

Y sobre hacer… he creado más de 10 proyectos distintos. Desde PetLoop (2022) hasta NutrIA y HeyMark hoy. He probado, fallado, ajustado y vuelto a intentar. Ese proceso me dio algo mucho más valioso que cualquier teoría: claridad.

Al salirme de la universidad, pude enfocar el 100% de mi tiempo en lo que realmente quería aprender. Y tuve suerte: desde chico tenía bastante claro lo que me gustaba (aunque también pasé por mi etapa de exploración… incluso fui DJ).

Hoy veo a mucha gente que sale de la universidad sin tener claro qué quiere hacer. Estudian 5 años algo general, luego enfrentan un mercado difícil, y recién ahí comienzan a explorar —tomándose 2 o 3 años más para entender qué les gusta de verdad.

Este es un llamado a la acción:

Si estás en la universidad, no te quedes solo con eso.
Haz cosas por fuera.
Explora.
Crea proyectos.

Da lo mismo si resultan o no. En el proceso vas a aprender, y más importante aún, vas a descubrir en qué quieres trabajar el resto de tu vida.

messages.content:
Desde que dejé la universidad, mi camino ha sido de mucho aprendizaje.



Tuve la oportunidad de trabajar con personas mucho más capacitadas que yo, y ahí entendí algo clave: hay muchas cosas que la universidad no te enseña. No voy a negar que al empezar tenía vacíos importantes, cosas que alguien titulado probablemente ya debería saber. Pero lo que no sabía, volvía a mi casa, lo aprendía y lo aplicaba al día siguiente.



Así ha sido siempre mi forma de formarme: aprender haciendo.



Y sobre hacer… he probado varios proyectos en estos años, desde PetLoop (2022) hasta NutrIA y HeyMark hoy. Muchos no salieron como esperaba, pero cada uno me enseñó algo. Creo que esa etapa de probar y equivocarse es clave para entender qué te gusta de verdad. Y una vez que lo tienes claro, lo más importante es ir con todo en eso.



Cuando tomé la decisión de salirme de la universidad, también me di cuenta de algo: encontrar trabajo no es fácil, y TODOS piden experiencia. Y ahí te das cuenta de algo más… ¿qué vale más realmente: tener un buen promedio, o haber creado proyectos, trabajado con gente y tenido usuarios reales?



Por eso creo que hay un error grande hoy. Mucha gente pasa 5 años estudiando algo general, sale sin experiencia real, y se enfrenta a un mercado exigente. Y peor aún, sin tener claro si eso era realmente lo que querían hacer. Eso los lleva a tomarse 2 o 3 años más para explorar desde cero.



En mi caso, al salirme pude enfocar el 100% de mi tiempo en lo que realmente quería aprender. Y tuve suerte: desde chico tenía bastante claro lo que me gustaba (aunque también pasé por mi etapa de exploración… incluso quería ser DJ).



Esto es un llamado a la acción, si estás en la universidad, no te quedes solo con eso. Aprovecha ese tiempo para construir experiencia real.

Haz cosas por fuera.

Explora.

content.text:
No puedo editar el guión directamente desde acá, pero está abierto en la station. Cópialo y reemplázalo:

---

**HOOK (5s)**
Cuando le dije a mi familia que dejaba la universidad para andar en bici y programar, nadie lo entendió.

---

**DEV (90s)**
Tuve la oportunidad de trabajar con personas mucho más capacitadas que yo, y ahí entendí algo clave: hay muchas cosas que la universidad no te enseña. No voy a negar que al empezar tenía vacíos importantes, cosas que alguien titulado ya debería saber. Pero lo que no sabía, volvía a mi casa, lo aprendía y lo aplicaba al día siguiente.

Así ha sido siempre mi forma de formarme: aprender haciendo.

He probado varios proyectos, desde PetLoop en 2022 hasta NutrIA y HeyMark hoy. Muchos no salieron como esperaba, pero cada uno me enseñó algo. Esa etapa de probar y equivocarse es clave para entender qué te gusta de verdad.

Cuando tomé la decisión de salirme, me di cuenta de algo: TODOS piden experiencia. Y en la práctica, la experiencia vale más que un buen promedio.

Mucha gente pasa 5 años estudiando algo general, sale sin experiencia real, y no tiene claro qué quiere. Eso los lleva a 2 o 3 años más de exploración desde cero.

Desde chico traté de explorar cosas, incluso fui DJ, hasta que entendí qué era lo que realmente me gustaba. Y una vez que lo tenés claro, enfocar tu tiempo en eso hace toda la diferencia.

---

**CTA (20s)**
Si estás en la universidad, no te quedes solo con eso. Haz cosas por fuera. Explora. Crea proyectos. Da lo mismo si resultan o no. En el proceso vas a descubrir en qué querés trabajar el resto de tu vida.

---

¿Cambiamos algo más?

content.text:
Ese texto negro es el gancho visual, lo que para el scroll antes de que suene el audio. Roy usa "my entire worldview in 45 seconds" porque es curioso y promete algo concreto en poco tiempo.

Para tu video, algunas opciones:

1. **"por qué dejé la universidad a los 20"** — directo, genera curiosidad, muy personal
2. **"lo que aprendí saliendo de la U"** — promesa de valor clara
3. **"mi decisión más difícil, en 2 minutos"** — crea tensión emocional
4. **"la experiencia vale más que el título"** — contrarian, genera debate
5. **"lo que nadie te dice sobre dejar la U"** — curiosity gap clásico

Mi recomendación: la **1**. Es la más tuya, la más específica y activa exactamente a los dos perfiles de tu audiencia. Los confundidos y los que ya eligieron.

¿Cuál te resuena?
